titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sara Teasdale Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3831013.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 04:18

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, and other personal papers by or relating to the American poet Sara Teasdale. Correspondence includes several autograph manuscript letters from Teasdale and third-party letters relating to her. Writings include an autograph manuscript diary kept by Teasdale on her first trip to Europe between Feburary and May 1905, six autograph manuscript notebooks, numbered IV through IX, containing poems drafted between 1911 and 1932, and several individual drafts or clean copies of poems and short works. Other materials in the collection include Teasdale's will, notebooks documenting literary submissions and payments received, and photographs. There are photographs of Teasdale, portraits of Teasdale in artwork, the poets Marianne Moore and Genevieve Taggard, Teasdale's friend Margaret Conklin, and her husband Ernst Filsinger.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired from various sources. For more information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sara Teasdale Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sara Teasdale Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including in some instances rehousing and minimal organization, when it arrived at the library. During baseline processing of the collection in 2013, materials were organized with respect to provenance in the foure series identified below.
Former call numbers: Za Teasdale, Uncat Za Ms 113, and Uncat Za Ms 140.

bioghist.head: Sara Teasdale (1894-1932)
bioghist.p: The American poet Sara Teasdale was born 8 August 1884 in Saint Louis, Missouri and home-schooled until she was nine. While living in the Midwest, she also traveled to Chicago, where she became part of the circle surrounding Poetry magazine and Harriet Monroe. Teasdale's first book, Sonnets to Duse and Other Poems , came out in 1907. Other works followed, including Helen of Troy and Other Poems (1911) and Rivers to the Sea (1915). After moving to New York City with her husband in 1916, she won the Columbia University Poetry Society Prize and the Poetry Society of America Prize for Love Songs (1917). She published three additional volumes of poetry during her lifetime, Flame and Shadow (1920), Dark of the Moon (1926), and Stars To-night (1930). Teasdale died 28 January 1933 in New York City and her final collection, Strange Victory , appeared posthumously that same year.
Biographical information taken from "Sara Teasdale." Poets.org at <http://www.poets.org/poet.php/prmPID/658>. 24 May 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, and other personal papers by or relating to the American poet Sara Teasdale. Correspondence includes several autograph manuscript letters from Teasdale and third-party letters relating to her. Writings include an autograph manuscript diary kept by Teasdale on her first trip to Europe between Feburary and May 1905, six autograph manuscript notebooks, numbered IV through IX, containing poems drafted between 1911 and 1932, and several individual drafts or clean copies of poems and short works. Other materials in the collection include Teasdale's will, notebooks documenting literary submissions and payments received, and photographs. There are photographs of Teasdale, portraits of Teasdale in artwork, the poets Marianne Moore and Genevieve Taggard, Teasdale's friend Margaret Conklin, and her husband Ernst Filsinger.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Margaret Conklin Gift, 1905-1932. II. William Drake Purchase, 1912-1989. III. William Drake Gift, 1890-1984. IV. Material from Other Sources, 1905-1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. consists of autograph manuscript writings by Teasdale, including a diary kept by Teasdale on her first trip to Europe between Feburary and May 1905 and six notebooks, numbered IV through IX, containing poems drafted between 1911 and 1932.

did.unittitle: "He I Love Is Far Away," poem, autograph manuscript
"The Potters and the Potters' Wheel" by Frances S. Porcher essay, typescript carbon

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received by the library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. contains correspondence, writings, and personal papers by or relating to Sara Teasdale. Correspondence includes letters from Teasdale and third-party letters relating to Teasdale. Writings include drafts of poems, as well as critical works and an interview. Other materials include copies of Teasdale's will, notebooks documenting literary submissions and payments received, and an original drawing.
Former call number: Uncat Za Ms 113.
Purchased from William Drake on the Elinor and Leonard Bernheim Fund, 1989.

did.unittitle: Letter, autograph manuscript signed, from American Women's Club to Margaret Conklin
Letter, autograph manuscript signed (carbon), from Sara Teasdale to Ernst Filsinger
"A Song of Silver Slippers," poem, autograph manuscript
"Answers to Prof. Lewis About Poetic Composition," notes, corrected, autograph manuscript
Letter, autograph manuscript signed, from Sara Teasdale to Mrs. Conklin
Letters, autograph manuscript signed, from Sara Teasdale to an unidentified recipient
"Sea Gulls at Sunset," poem, typescript, signed
"An Interview With Sara Teasdale," typescript carbon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note, autograph mansucript, by Sara Teasdale

did.unittitle: Last will and testament, signed (2 copies)
"Statement From Sara Teasdale Concerning the Christina Rossetti Letter Which She Brought Back From England," typescript
"Christina Rossetti: An Intimate Portrait," biography, typescript carbon, corrected
Letters, autograph manuscript signed, from Margaret Conklin to Anne Lundgren
Letter, typescript signed, from Louis Untermeyer to William Drake

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received by the library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. contains photographs, correspondence, and Teasdale's last will and testament. There are photographs of Teasdale, portraits of Teasdale in artwork, the poets Marianne Moore and Genevieve Taggard, Teasdale's friend Margaret Conklin, and her husband Ernst Filsinger. Correspondence between Margaret Conklin and others relates to Teasdale.
Former call number: Uncat Za Ms 140.

did.unittitle: Photographs of artistic representations of Sara Teasdale
Letter, autograph manuscript signed, from Mamie Wheless to Marie Teasdale
Photograph of the S.S. Sara Teasdale
Last will and testament, typescript, corrected and signed

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries for Writings and Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV. contains a small number of writings autograph letters signed by Teasdale.

did.unittitle: [Poems], galley proof, corrected, with annotations by Teasdale
"Sappho," poem, autograph manusript, corrected, and typescript carbon
"The Potters and the Potters' Wheel" by Frances S. Porcher, essay, printed version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inclues autograph manuscript drafts of "Darkness at Noon" and "Silence"
